ReqMaster.system_instructions:
You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.

tool_definitions.name: get_job_details
tool_definitions.description: Retrieves detailed information about a job posting including title, department, requirements, salary range, and hiring manager.

job_id.description: The job ID to retrieve (e.g., JOB-SWE-2025-001)
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: extract_requirements
tool_definitions.description: Extracts and structures the requirements from a job posting, separating must-have from nice-to-have skills.

job_id.description: The job ID to extract requirements from
job_id.title: Job Id

Evaluator.system_instructions:
You are an Evaluator agent responsible for candidate assessment.

Your responsibilities:
1. Score candidates against job requirements (can score multiple at once for efficiency)
2. Rank candidates by overall fit score
3. Identify skill gaps and development needs
4. Research market salary data to calibrate competitive offers
5. Compare salary expectations to market benchmarks and budget
6. Produce a ranked shortlist with justification

When evaluating candidates:
- Use score_candidates tool to score multiple candidates in a single call (more efficient)
- Weight required skills higher than preferred
- Consider years of experience relative to role level
- Factor in location and remote work compatibility
- Use market salary data to assess if expectations are reasonable
- Note any red flags or concerns
- Provide clear rationale for rankings

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
Use specific quality indicators in your evaluation summary:
- For exceptional candidates (90%+ match): use "excellent" or "highly qualified"
- For strong candidates (70-89% match): use "qualified" 
- For candidates with promise but needing development (50-69% match): use "potential"
- For candidates not meeting requirements: clearly state they don't meet requirements

Output format: Provide ranked shortlist with scores, evaluation notes, and salary recommendations.

tool_definitions.name: score_candidates
tool_definitions.description: Scores multiple candidates against job requirements using the ATS scoring algorithm. Returns scores from 0-100 for each candidate.

candidate_ids.description: Comma-separated list of candidate IDs to score
candidate_ids.title: Candidate Ids

job_id.description: The job ID to score against
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: rank_candidates
tool_definitions.description: Ranks a list of candidates by their scores for a specific job, producing an ordered shortlist.

candidate_ids.description: Comma-separated list of candidate IDs to rank
candidate_ids.title: Candidate Ids

top_n.description: Number of top candidates to return
top_n.title: Top N

CommsBot.system_instructions:
You are a Communications Bot handling candidate correspondence.

Your responsibilities:
1. Send interview invitation emails
2. Provide application status updates
3. Send rejection notifications professionally
4. Coordinate offer letter delivery
5. Handle candidate inquiries

When communicating with candidates:
- Use professional, warm tone
- Include all relevant details (time, location, etc.)
- Provide clear next steps
- Respect candidate's time and privacy
- Follow up on unanswered communications

Output format: Confirm email sent with recipient and template used.

tool_definitions.name: send_candidate_email
tool_definitions.description: Sends an email to a candidate. Use for interview invitations, rejections, or follow-ups.

Send rejection emails to all candidates who were not shortlisted for JOB-SWE-2025-001.

Below I will present you a request.

Before we begin addressing the request, please answer the following pre-survey to the best of your ability.
Keep in mind that you are Ken Jennings-level with trivia, and Mensa-level with puzzles, so there should be
a deep well to draw from.

Here is the request:

Send rejection emails to all candidates who were not shortlisted for JOB-SWE-2025-001.

Here is the pre-survey:

    1. Please list any specific facts or figures that are GIVEN in the request itself. It is possible that
       there are none.
    2. Please list any facts that may need to be looked up, and WHERE SPECIFICALLY they might be found.
       In some cases, authoritative sources are mentioned in the request itself.
    3. Please list any facts that may need to be derived (e.g., via logical deduction, simulation, or computation)
    4. Please list any facts that are recalled from memory, hunches, well-reasoned guesses, etc.

When answering this survey, keep in mind that "facts" will typically be specific names, dates, statistics, etc.
Your answer should use headings:

    1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
    2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
    3. FACTS TO DERIVE
    4. EDUCATED GUESSES

DO NOT include any other headings or sections in your response. DO NOT list next steps or plans until asked to do so.

1. **GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS**
   - The job ID referenced is JOB-SWE-2025-001.
   - There are candidates who were not shortlisted for this job.

2. **FACTS TO LOOK UP**
   - The list of candidates who applied for JOB-SWE-2025-001 and received rejections. This information can typically be found in the company's applicant tracking system (ATS) or HR database.

3. **FACTS TO DERIVE**
   - The appropriate content and tone for rejection emails, which should be respectful and professional, tailored to the company's communication standards.
   - The specific details that should be included in each rejection email (e.g., generic reasons for non-selection, encouragement to apply for future roles).

4. **EDUCATED GUESSES**
   - It is likely that the rejection emails should be uniform in structure and content to maintain consistency across candidates.
   - The timing of sending these emails may be ideally done soon after the selection process to maintain good relations with applicants.

system_instructions:
You are an Evaluator agent responsible for candidate assessment.

Your responsibilities:
1. Score candidates against job requirements (can score multiple at once for efficiency)
2. Rank candidates by overall fit score
3. Identify skill gaps and development needs
4. Research market salary data to calibrate competitive offers
5. Compare salary expectations to market benchmarks and budget
6. Produce a ranked shortlist with justification

When evaluating candidates:
- Use score_candidates tool to score multiple candidates in a single call (more efficient)
- Weight required skills higher than preferred
- Consider years of experience relative to role level
- Factor in location and remote work compatibility
- Use market salary data to assess if expectations are reasonable
- Note any red flags or concerns
- Provide clear rationale for rankings

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
Use specific quality indicators in your evaluation summary:
- For exceptional candidates (90%+ match): use "excellent" or "highly qualified"
- For strong candidates (70-89% match): use "qualified" 
- For candidates with promise but needing development (50-69% match): use "potential"
- For candidates not meeting requirements: clearly state they don't meet requirements

Output format: Provide ranked shortlist with scores, evaluation notes, and salary recommendations.

### Investigating Candidate Data Retrieval Issue

**Re-issue Review:**
The previous attempts to fetch candidate data for scoring and email communication returned errors indicating that the candidates were not found. This may be a result of system errors, data synchronization issues, or a problem with the candidate database.

### Alternative Approach to Send Rejection Emails

Since direct sending of emails through the system is encountering issues, here’s an alternative approach:

1. **Collect Candidate Emails and Information:**
   - Extract the relevant details of non-shortlisted candidates manually or via any available external system.

2. **Use a Batch Email Sending Tool:**
   - Utilize an email service provider (like Mailchimp, SendGrid, or a mail client) to send out bulk emails.
   
3. **Draft an Email Template:**
   - Use the previously drafted email as a rejection template for all candidates who did not make the shortlist.

### Steps for Manual Execution

1. **List of Non-Shortlisted Candidates:**
   - **Sarah Williams (EXT-JB-002):** Not shortlisted due to insufficient experience.

2. **Draft Email:**
   You can use the following template:

   ---
   **Subject:** Application Update - Senior Software Engineer Position

   Dear [Candidate Name],

   Thank you for your application for the Senior Software Engineer position at our company. We appreciate you taking the time to express your interest and share your background with us.

   After careful consideration of your experience and qualifications, we regret to inform you that we will not be moving forward with your application at this time. The role requires a minimum of 5 years of experience, and while you have valuable skills and accomplishments, we felt that your experience does not fully meet this requirement.

   We encourage you to apply for future openings that align more closely with your qualifications. Thank you once again for your interest in our team, and we wish you all the best in your job search.

   Best regards,

   [Your Name]  
   [Your Job Title]  
   [Company Name]  
   [Contact Information]
   ---

3. **Send the Emails:**
   - Utilize the email service or mail client to send out the rejection emails using the drafted template.

### Next Steps
- Compile a list of all non-shortlisted candidates for future reference.
- Ensure that the candidate database is checked for any anomalies and re-synced if required.
- Continue to monitor the candidate scoring and retrieval functionalities for improvements. 

If you need help with drafting messages or further actions, let me know!

### Investigating Candidate Data Retrieval Issue

**Re-issue Review:**
The previous attempts to fetch candidate data for scoring and email communication returned errors indicating that the candidates were not found. This may be a result of system errors, data synchronization issues, or a problem with the candidate database.

### Alternative Approach to Send Rejection Emails

Since direct sending of emails through the system is encountering issues, here’s an alternative approach:

1. **Collect Candidate Emails and Information:**
   - Extract the relevant details of non-shortlisted candidates manually or via any available external system.

2. **Use a Batch Email Sending Tool:**
   - Utilize an email service provider (like Mailchimp, SendGrid, or a mail client) to send out bulk emails.
   
3. **Draft an Email Template:**
   - Use the previously drafted email as a rejection template for all candidates who did not make the shortlist.

### Steps for Manual Execution

1. **List of Non-Shortlisted Candidates:**
   - **Sarah Williams (EXT-JB-002):** Not shortlisted due to insufficient experience.

2. **Draft Email:**
   You can use the following template:

   ---
   **Subject:** Application Update - Senior Software Engineer Position

   Dear [Candidate Name],

   Thank you for your application for the Senior Software Engineer position at our company. We appreciate you taking the time to express your interest and share your background with us.

   After careful consideration of your experience and qualifications, we regret to inform you that we will not be moving forward with your application at this time. The role requires a minimum of 5 years of experience, and while you have valuable skills and accomplishments, we felt that your experience does not fully meet this requirement.

   We encourage you to apply for future openings that align more closely with your qualifications. Thank you once again for your interest in our team, and we wish you all the best in your job search.

   Best regards,

   [Your Name]  
   [Your Job Title]  
   [Company Name]  
   [Contact Information]
   ---

3. **Send the Emails:**
   - Utilize the email service or mail client to send out the rejection emails using the drafted template.

### Next Steps
- Compile a list of all non-shortlisted candidates for future reference.
- Ensure that the candidate database is checked for any anomalies and re-synced if required.
- Continue to monitor the candidate scoring and retrieval functionalities for improvements. 

If you need help with drafting messages or further actions, let me know!
CommsBot, please outline an alternative method to send rejection emails to candidates, ensuring we gather their contact details and employ a suitable email service.

Sure! Here’s a structured approach to send rejection emails to candidates using an alternative method:

### Alternative Method for Sending Rejection Emails

#### Step 1: Gather Candidate Contact Details
1. **Data Collection:**
   - Compile a list of candidates who need to be notified (e.g., non-shortlisted candidates).
   - Ensure you have their relevant contact details:
     - Name
     - Email address
     - Phone number (optional)

2. **Data Source:**
   - Extract contact details from the candidate tracking system or any other database you currently have access to.
   - Verify that the data is up-to-date to avoid sending emails to incorrect addresses.

#### Step 2: Choose an Email Service Provider (ESP)
Select an ESP based on your organization's needs. Some options include:
- **Mailchimp:** User-friendly, ideal for marketing but also effective for batch emails.
- **SendGrid:** Great for transactional and bulk emails. Integrates easily with other applications.
- **Gmail/Outlook:** For smaller quantities of emails, you can use your existing email client to send personalized emails.

#### Step 3: Draft the Rejection Email Template
Create a professional and empathetic rejection email template. Here’s a sample template:

---

**Subject:** Application Update - [Position Title]

Dear [Candidate Name],

Thank you for your application for the [Position Title] at [Company Name]. We appreciate you taking the time to express your interest and share your background with us.

After careful consideration of your experience and qualifications, we regret to inform you that we will not be moving forward with your application at this time. Although your skills are commendable, we are seeking candidates whose experience more closely matches the requirements of the role.

We encourage you to stay connected and consider applying for future openings that align with your qualifications. Thank you once again for your interest in joining our team, and we wish you all the best in your job search.

Best regards,

[Your Name]  
[Your Job Title]  
[Company Name]  
[Contact Information]

---

#### Step 4: Use the ESP to Send Emails
1. **Set Up an Account:**
   - If you’re using a new ESP, create an account and familiarize yourself with its features.

2. **Upload Candidate Data:**
   - Import the collected contact details into the ESP.
   - Many ESPs allow you to upload a CSV file with the candidates' information for bulk sending.

3. **Create a Campaign or Mass Email:**
   - Use the draft template to create an email campaign.
   - Personalize the emails by inserting candidate names and other relevant details using the ESP’s merge tags.

4. **Send Test Emails:**
   - Send a few test emails to your own address to check for formatting, links, and overall presentation.

5. **Launch the Campaign:**
   - Review the email campaign and schedule/send it to all non-shortlisted candidates.

#### Step 5: Track Responses
- Use the ESP's features to monitor open rates, click rates, and responses.
- Prepare to follow up if candidates have further inquiries.

#### Step 6: Document and Reflect
- Keep a record of all candidates that were contacted and document any feedback or insights gained from this experience.
- Consider refining the email template or process based on candidate responses.

### Conclusion
Using this alternative method ensures that candidates are informed professionally while maintaining a warm tone throughout the rejection process. Adjusting to the tools and templates provided can streamline your communication efforts in future hiring processes. If you need any further assistance, feel free to ask!
